characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A successful executive at Radford Studio, husband to Kim, brother-in-law to Jimmy and Christine, father to Sammy (and Emily after her inclusion in the family), and uncle to Dominic and Logan. Abnormal Allergy: In "We're Having a Baby", it is revealed by Tom and Natalie that Greg is allergic to a large number of things. In addition to the standard allergies of strawberries, peanuts, pollen, shellfish, and dander, he is also allergic to dryer sheets, down pillows, scented candles, and will-based colognes. Natalie believes all those allergies to have developed as a result of her never breastfeeding him as a child (as back then, people didn't breastfeed that much). Butt-Monkey: A lot of drama in this series happens as the result of things not going the way he wants them to go. As revealed in some episodes, he had it rough in his younger years as well. Deadpan Snarker: One of the most proficient characters in this series to make sarcastic quips. Henpecked Husband: To Kim.

characters.name: Gregory "Greg" Warner
characters.description: A successful executive at Radford Studio, husband to Kim, brother-in-law to Jimmy and Christine, father to Sammy (and Emily after her inclusion in the family), and uncle to Dominic and Logan. Abnormal Allergy: In "We're Having a Baby", it is revealed by Tom and Natalie that Greg is allergic to a large number of things. In addition to the standard allergies of strawberries, peanuts, pollen, shellfish, and dander, he is also allergic to dryer sheets, down pillows, scented candles, and will-based colognes. Natalie believes all those allergies to have developed as a result of her never breastfeeding him as a child (as back then, people didn't breastfeed that much). Butt-Monkey: A lot of drama in this series happens as the result of things not going the way he wants them to go. As revealed in some episodes, he had it rough in his younger years as well. Deadpan Snarker: One of the most proficient characters in this series to make sarcastic quips. Henpecked Husband: To Kim.

characters.name: Kimberly "Kim" Warner (née Ludke)
characters.description: Greg's wife, a stay-at-home mother to Sammy (and Emily after her birth), and Dominic and Logan's aunt. All Women Are Prudes: As revealed in "Kiss and Yell", the amount of boys she kissed before settling down with Greg is seven. She thinks that's a high number. Housewife: Her de facto roles are to maintain the household upkeep and raise child(ren).

characters.name: Emily Warner
characters.description: Greg and Kim's daughter. Cousin Oliver: She becomes an additional member of the Warner family during Season 3. Living Prop: Her role is almost exclusively to just be present. Among the children in this series, she has the fewest speaking lines and the least amount of plot relevance post-birth.

characters.name: James "Jimmy" Hughes, Jr.
characters.description: Husband to Christine, brother-in-law to Greg and Kim, father to Dominic and Logan, and uncle to Sammy (and Emily after her birth). Initially unemployed, he becomes hired as a security guard at Radford Studio. Big Man on Campus: He wasvery popularin his high school years. Book Dumb: Though he is bad at studying, in the everyday life he is much wiser than he seems to be. Bumbling Dad: While he means well and tries hard to be a good father to his sons, he's comically incompetent at times. Future Loser: He wasvery popular in high school, attended college but dropped out soon afterward, and, for the most of the series' run, lives (along with his children) in a guesthouse belonging to Christine's sister and her successful husband.

characters.name: Christine Hughes (née Ludke)
characters.description: Wife to Jimmy, mother to Dominic and Logan, sister-in-law to Greg, and aunt to Sammy (and Emily after her birth). All Girls Want Bad Boys: She was drawn to Jimmy'sBig Man on Campusstatus in high school. Back to School: She starts going back to college in "Christine's Journey" and her status as an adult in college has remained for the rest of the series. Deadpan Snarker: She's about just as capable of making snarky remarks as Greg, sans thePerpetual Frownerfacial expression. Hard-Drinking Party Girl: She was one in her high school days and is still one in the present when given an opportunity, if rarely nowadays due to her responsibilities as an adult.

characters.name: Dominic Hughes
characters.description: Jimmy and Christine's older son. Dirty Kid: He harbors an infatuation towards older women, most prominently Kim, in some episodes.

characters.name: Logan Hughes
characters.description: Jimmy and Christine's younger son. Embarrassing First Name: "House of Cards" reveals that his official birth-name is actually "Lobo" because Christine was sedated when she stated her decision on Logan's name and Jimmy misunderstood her, though this information is known only to Jimmy (and Greg once Jimmy relays it to him).

characters.name: Tom Warner
characters.description: Father to Greg, father-in-law to Kim, and grandfather to Sammy (and Emily later on). Bumbling Dad: Not as frequent an example as Jimmy (and certainly less so than Big Jimmy), but he has his moments.

characters.name: Natalie Warner
characters.description: Mother to Greg, mother-in-law to Kim, and grandmother to Sammy (and Emily later on). Obnoxious In-Laws: To Kim, for she's not shy with criticizing Kim about what she disapproves Kim of whenever such an occasion arises.

characters.name: James "Big Jimmy" Hughes, Sr.
characters.description: Father to Jimmy, father-in-law to Christine, and grandfather to Dominic and Logan. Bumbling Dad:Even worseat childcare than his son is.

characters.name: Kitty Hughes
characters.description: Mother to Jimmy, mother-in-law to Christine, and grandmother to Dominic and Logan. Smoking Is Not Cool: She's almost always shown smoking a cigarette. Her inelegant behaviors do nothing to make smoking appealing.

characters.name: Don Ludke
characters.description: Father to Kim and Christine, father-in-law to Greg and Jimmy, and grandfather to all of their children. Obnoxious In-Laws: To Greg, as he tends to take jabs at Greg. Volleying Insults: His default communication with Jenny, his ex-wife, tends to involve him claiming to be glad to be free from her and her claiming to have half of his assets in return.

characters.name: Jenny Ludke
characters.description: Mother to Kim and Christine, mother-in-law to Greg and Jimmy, and grandmother to all of their children. Volleying Insults: Her default communication with Don, her ex-husband, tends to involve him claiming to be glad to be free from her and her claiming to have half of his assets in return.

characters.name: Billy Colivita
characters.description: A friend of Jimmy's since their younger years.

characters.name: Roy Barr
characters.description: A fellow security guard at Radford Studio. Token Minority: The only non-white (in his case, black) character in the main and recurring cast lineup.

characters.name: Mr. George Savitsky
characters.description: President of Radford Studio. Benevolent Boss: Despite all the wacky requests and orders he has tasked Greg with, he's aReasonable Authority Figureall the same. Serial Spouse: He's shown being married with at least two different wives (obviously not at the same time) throughout the series.
